q: Failure is the key to success; each mistake teaches us something.
h:
“Failure is the key to success; each mistake teaches us something.” —
Morihei Ueshiba

q: The wisdom of life consists in the elimination of the nonessentials.
h:
“The wisdom of life consists in the elimination of the nonessentials.” —
Lin Yutang

q: You don't have to control your thoughts; you just have to stop letting them control you.
h:
“You don't have to control your thoughts; you just have to stop letting them control you.” —
Dan Millman

q: Many people will walk in and out of your life, but only true friends will leave footprints in your heart.
h:
“Many people will walk in and out of your life, but only true friends will leave footprints in your heart.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: Human happiness and moral duty are inseparably connected.
h:
“Human happiness and moral duty are inseparably connected. ” —
George Washington

q: A clear conscience is the sure sign of a bad memory.
h:
“A clear conscience is the sure sign of a bad memory.” —
Mark Twain

q: Just do the best you can. No one can do more than that.
h:
“Just do the best you can. No one can do more than that.” —
John Wooden

q: Don't cry because it's over. Smile because it happened.
h:
“Don't cry because it's over. Smile because it happened.” —
Dr. Seuss

q: He who hesitates is a damned fool.
h:
“He who hesitates is a damned fool.” —
Mae West

q: Judge your success by what you had to give up in order to get it.
h:
“Judge your success by what you had to give up in order to get it.” —
Dalai Lama

q: Fallacies do not cease to be fallacies because they become fashions.
h:
“Fallacies do not cease to be fallacies because they become fashions.” —
Gilbert Chesterton

q: Reflect upon your present blessings of which every man has many - not on your past misfortunes, of which all men have some.
h:
“Reflect upon your present blessings of which every man has many - not on your past misfortunes, of which all men have some.” —
Charles Dickens

q: You can't have anything worthwhile without difficulties.
h:
“You can't have anything worthwhile without difficulties.” —
Harry S. Truman

q: It is our experiences that mold us into who we are...during times of adversity our true character will show.
h:
“It is our experiences that mold us into who we are...during times of adversity our true character will show.” —
Unknown

q: I am always doing what I cannot do yet, in order to learn how to do it.
h:
“I am always doing what I cannot do yet, in order to learn how to do it.” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: Only when you can be extremely pliable and soft can you be extremely hard and strong.
h:
“Only when you can be extremely pliable and soft can you be extremely hard and strong.” —
Zen Proverb

q: If you're afraid - don't do it, - if you're doing it - don't be afraid!
h:
“If you're afraid - don't do it, - if you're doing it - don't be afraid! ” —
Genghis Khan

q: Everything will be ok in the end. If it's not ok, it's not the end.
h:
“Everything will be ok in the end. If it's not ok, it's not the end.” —
John Lennon

q: It is passion that makes man live; wisdom makes one only last.
h:
“It is passion that makes man live; wisdom makes one only last.” —
Nicolas Chamfort

q: If you want others to be happy, practice compassion. If you want to be happy, practice compassion.
h:
“If you want others to be happy, practice compassion. If you want to be happy, practice compassion.” —
Dalai Lama

q: If you do not get the chills when you set your goal you're not setting big enough goals.
h:
“If you do not get the chills when you set your goal you're not setting big enough goals.” —
Bob Proctor

q: If you cling to a certain thought with dynamic will power, it finally assumes a tangible outward form.
h:
“If you cling to a certain thought with dynamic will power, it finally assumes a tangible outward form.” —
Paramahansa Yogananda

q: The world doesn't always give you want, but it often gives you what you need.
h:
“The world doesn't always give you want, but it often gives you what you need.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: The less you try to impress, the more peaceful you can be.
h:
“The less you try to impress, the more peaceful you can be.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: Quality is not an act, it is a habit.
h:
“Quality is not an act, it is a habit.” —
Aristotle

q: What you are afraid of is never as bad as what you imagine. The fear you let build up in your mind is worse than the situation that actually exists.
h:
“What you are afraid of is never as bad as what you imagine. The fear you let build up in your mind is worse than the situation that actually exists.” —
Spencer Johnson

q: Fishing is much more than fish.
h:
“Fishing is much more than fish.” —
Herbert Hoover

q: If you want to keep your memories, you first have to live them.
h:
“If you want to keep your memories, you first have to live them.” —
Bob Dylan

q: Life is a traveling to the edge of knowledge, then a leap taken.
h:
“Life is a traveling to the edge of knowledge, then a leap taken.” —
D. H. Lawrence

q: If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it well enough.
h:
“If you can't explain it simply, you don't understand it well enough.” —
Albert Einstein

q: Whatever your heart clings to and confides in, that is really your God.
h:
“Whatever your heart clings to and confides in, that is really your God.” —
Martin Luther

q: Even if our home burns down we can rebuild it. But the things that we got for nothing, we can never replace.
h:
“Even if our home burns down we can rebuild it. But the things that we got for nothing, we can never replace.” —
Earl Nightingale

q: Most people do not really want freedom, because freedom involves responsibility, and most people are frightened of responsibility.
h:
“Most people do not really want freedom, because freedom involves responsibility, and most people are frightened of responsibility.” —
Sigmund Freud

q: To the mind that is still, the whole universe surrenders.
h:
“To the mind that is still, the whole universe surrenders.” —
Lao Tzu

q: Be who you are and say what you feel because those who mind don't matter and those who matter don't mind.
h:
“Be who you are and say what you feel because those who mind don't matter and those who matter don't mind.” —
Dr. Seuss

q: Unexpressed emotions will never die. They are buried alive and will come forth later in uglier ways.
h:
“Unexpressed emotions will never die. They are buried alive and will come forth later in uglier ways.” —
Sigmund Freud

q: Belief creates the actual fact.
h:
“Belief creates the actual fact.” —
William James

q: The happiest people in the world are those who feel absolutely terrific about themselves.
h:
“The happiest people in the world are those who feel absolutely terrific about themselves.” —
Brian Tracy

q: Every experience is a lesson. Every loss is a gain.
h:
“Every experience is a lesson. Every loss is a gain.” —
Sathya Sai Baba

q: If you put yourself in a position where you have to stretch outside your comfort zone, then you are forced to expand your consciousness.
h:
“If you put yourself in a position where you have to stretch outside your comfort zone, then you are forced to expand your consciousness.” —
Unknown

q: Life is not just the passing of time. Life is the collection of experiences and their intensity.
h:
“Life is not just the passing of time. Life is the collection of experiences and their intensity.” —
Jim Rohn

q: Throw your heart over the fence and the rest will follow
h:
“Throw your heart over the fence and the rest will follow” —
Norman Vincent Peale

q: Be yourself; everyone else is already taken.
h:
“Be yourself; everyone else is already taken.” —
Oscar Wilde

q: The mind is the limit.
h:
“The mind is the limit.” —
Arnold Schwarzenegger

q: Always render more and better service than is expected of you, no matter what your task may be.
h:
“Always render more and better service than is expected of you, no matter what your task may be.” —
Og Mandino

q: Happiness cannot be traveled to, owned, earned, worn or consumed.
h:
“Happiness cannot be traveled to, owned, earned, worn or consumed.” —
Denis Waitley

q: The real art in learning takes place as we move beyond proficiency, when our work becomes an expression of our essence.
h:
“The real art in learning takes place as we move beyond proficiency, when our work becomes an expression of our essence.” —
Josh Waitzkin

q: Friendship is one of the most tangible things in a world which offers fewer and fewer supports.
h:
“Friendship is one of the most tangible things in a world which offers fewer and fewer supports.” —
Kenneth Branagh

q: No idea, no matter how good, will work if it doesn't get used.
h:
“No idea, no matter how good, will work if it doesn't get used.” —
Peter A. Cohen

q: We have no more right to consume happiness without producing it than to consume wealth without producing it.
h:
“We have no more right to consume happiness without producing it than to consume wealth without producing it.” —
George Bernard Shaw
